title: pelargonium hypoleucum
intro: Pelargonium hypoleucum, also known as the Barberton daisy or the Mountain geranium, is a plant species native to the southern parts of Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal. The health benefits of Pelargonium hypoleucum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Pelargonium hypoleucum include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent.
